description_long: This object is a vintage military-style radio receiver setup placed on a worn wooden table. The central unit is a large, boxy metal radio receiver with a front panel featuring numerous dials, switches, and indicator lights, as well as a circular emblem or medallion at the top. To the left, there is a classic adjustable desk lamp and several scattered papers and books, while to the right, a smaller metal box and a cylindrical device are present, likely auxiliary radio components. The radio receiver's front panel is detailed with various controls, suggesting manual tuning and operation. The entire setup has an industrial, utilitarian appearance, with a dark olive-green color dominating the metal surfaces. The scene evokes a historical or wartime communication station, complete with period-appropriate accessories.
description: A vintage military radio receiver setup with a large metal main unit, dials, switches, and auxiliary components on a worn wooden table. The scene includes a desk lamp, books, and scattered papers, evoking a historical communication station.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth of the main radio receiver, two-drawer file cabinet, lamp, books, and scattered papers.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the detailed control panel of the radio receiver, dials, switches, and the arrangement of auxiliary components and lamp.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the cylindrical auxiliary device, the file cabinet, lamp, and more scattered papers.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the backs of the radio receiver and auxiliary box, with books and lamp visible on the table.
